footer.tagline: Creative writer and critic exploring the intersections of film, theater, and cultural storytelling.

home.heroTagline: Creative writer and critic focused on film, theater, and cultural storytelling.
home.heroIntro: I explore how stories are told—on screen, on stage, and in culture at large. Through reviews, essays, and editorial projects, I examine the craft behind the narratives that shape how we see the world.
home.featuredSubtitle: Film and theater reviews, plus video essays and critical video work.
home.quote: The best criticism doesn't just evaluate—it illuminates. It reveals what we felt but couldn't name.
home.ctaSubtitle: Whether it's a freelance assignment, editorial opportunity, or a conversation about cinema—I'd love to hear from you.

about.title: The Story Behind the Criticism
about.bio1: I'm Sergiu Cojocari—a writer and critic working at the intersection of film, theater, and cultural storytelling. Based between New York and London, I spend my time watching, thinking, and writing about how stories move us.
about.bio2: My work has appeared in The Criterion Observer, IndieWire, Curtain Call Magazine, and Theater Today. I also publish "Reel & Stage," a weekly newsletter reaching nearly 5,000 subscribers who share my fascination with narrative craft.
about.bio3: Whether I'm reviewing a debut feature at a film festival, analyzing a bold new theatrical production, or exploring a cultural trend through an editorial series, my goal is always the same: to help people see more clearly what makes a story resonate.
about.valuesSubtitle: The principles that guide how I engage with art and storytelling.
about.value1Desc: I believe criticism should be honest and exacting, but never cruel. Great analysis invites readers in rather than shutting them out.
about.value2Desc: I'm drawn to work that challenges, surprises, and resists easy categorization. The most interesting stories are often the ones that don't fit neatly.
about.value3Desc: Art doesn't exist in a vacuum. I situate every review and essay within its cultural, historical, and social context.
about.value4Desc: Complex ideas deserve clear language. I write for anyone who cares about stories, not just for other critics.

projects.pageSubtitle: Movie reviews and video essays—filter by type below.

1.title: Review: How Silence Drives Tension in Modern Psychological Cinema
1.description: An in-depth analysis of sound design choices in contemporary psychological thrillers, exploring how directors use silence as a narrative tool.

5.title: Video Essay: The Visual Language of Wong Kar-wai
5.description: A 15-minute video essay dissecting the cinematographic techniques, color palettes, and emotional rhythms in Wong Kar-wai's filmography.

contact.thankYouSub: I'll get back to you soon.
